CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-08-2015 Continued To Dec-16-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant And Cross-Complainant Flora Ongs Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Causes Of Actions (C.C.P. 425.16)
Off Calendar Oct-27-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant And Cross-Complainant Flora Ongs' Special Motion To Strike Causes Of Actions (C.C.P. 425.16) - The Anti-SLAPP motion is granted in part and denied in part. Although Plaintiff filed her anti-SLAPP motion approximately two weeks after the statutory deadline, it is within the court's discretion to consider and TO grant a late filed anti-SLAPP motion on the merits even if the defendant failed to request leave of court to file an untimely motion. (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 425.16(f); Chitsazzadeh v. Kramer & Kasslow (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 676, 684.) Defendant's anti-SLAPP motion, as it pertains to the first cause of action is granted. Defendant has established that the customer review comments, which she posted on the Care.com, are protected speech under Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 425.16(b)(1). Here, Defendant wrote her comments on a public website that invites negative and positive reviews of individuals registered on that website. The comments concern a public issue because they are available for other potential consumers to read and to help the customers decide if they want to use the services provided by the reviewed service providers. (See Willbanks v. Wolk (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 883.) Plaintiff cannot prevail on the first cause of action because she has not alleged that she has suffered special damages. Plaintiff cannot have it both ways. She cannot argue that Defendant's statement that Plaintiff, "Had no experience professionally pet sitting," is defamatory because it implies that she lied about being a professional pet sitter in order to secure the job, and then also argue that she is entitled to recovery under a libel per se theory on the basis that the statements have harmed her business and professional reputation. The anti-SLAPP motion, as it pertains to the second cause of action, is denied. Plaintiff has submitted evidence which indicates that there is a probability of prevailing on the second cause of action. (Cal. Code Civ. Proc. 425.16(b)(1).) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

Defendant And Cross-Complainant Flora Ongs Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Causes Of Actions (C.C.P. 425.16)
Off Calendar Sep-16-2015 Continued To Oct-27-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FLORA ONG'S Special Motion To Strike Causes Of Actions (C.C.P. 425.16) is continued to October 27, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion, to give all parties a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. All parties must lodge courtesy copies of their filings with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)
